Scam Alert: Fake email on ‘Tuition Fee Outstanding Payment’ is circulating at other institutions. Unsure? Call ScamShield 1799 anytime or check scamshield.gov.sg to stay safe.

Upcoming Events

Conferences & Seminars

4th Annual Symposium ― Mycobacteria 2026: Translating Biology into Cures

25 May 2026 - 26 May 2026
Conferences & Seminars

[PhD Oral Defence] Nanocondensation of a Type III Effector XopR Subverts Plant Immunity by Xie Yi

17 Mar 2026 09.30 AM - 11.30 AM

Past Events

  • 6th Singapore Malaria Network (SingMalNet) Meeting 2026

    12 February, 2026-14 February, 2026
    The 6th meeting of the Singapore Malaria Network intends to foster new ideas and concepts by bringing together scientists working in multiple aspects of malaria.

  • Singapore Bioscience Symposium (SBS) 2025

    10 December, 2025-12 December, 2025
    The inaugural Singapore Bioscience Symposium (SBS) 2025 will spotlight the emerging topic of Plant Biomolecular Condensation and Signalling.

  • 3rd Annual Symposium: Innovations in anti-mycobacterial Drug Discovery

    27 May, 2024-28 May, 2024
    The 3rd Annual Symposium: Innovations in anti-mycobacterial Drug Discovery will include presentations from speakers from around the world who have made significant advances in TB- and NTM research and –drug discovery. The goal of this event is to present novel concepts and to provide a platform for vibrant and open discussions between industry, senior scientists, postdoctoral fellows and students from academia. The symposium is organized by members of TELMabNet (Targeting energy of life for the development of drug combinations to eradicate antibiotic-tolerant Mycobacterium abscessus, a clinical nightmare, Network), an excellence cluster funded by the Singapore National Research Foundation.

  • Plant Biomechanics for Sustainable Agriculture and Forestry

    20 May, 2024-22 May, 2024
    Understanding the mechanical properties of plants and their response to environmental factors is essential for developing innovative and sustainable solutions to address the challenges facing agriculture and forestry. 'Plant Biomechanics for Sustainable Agriculture and Forestry’, co-organized by Nanyang Technological University-Singapore, and Zhejiang Agriculture & Forestry University-China, will delve into the critical intersection of plant biomechanics and its impact on sustainable agriculture and forestry. The conference scheduled to take place from May 19-22, 2024 in Singapore at NTU. The event will feature a distinguished lineup of Plenary Speakers and host presentations from invited speakers representing prestigious institutions, ensuring a comprehensive and diverse range of perspectives. This conference aims to foster collaboration, knowledge exchange, and networking opportunities among researchers, scientists, and industry professionals working in the field of plant biomechanics. It will provide a platform for the dissemination of the latest research findings, innovative methodologies, and sustainable practices that contribute to the advancement of agriculture and forestry. We invite all participants to engage in this enriching and rewarding experience, with the goal of fostering meaningful discussions and collaborations that will contribute to the sustainable future of agriculture and forestry. We look forward to welcoming you to this important gathering and to the valuable insights and contributions you will bring to the conference. The workshop is funded by the Zhejiang Agriculture & Forestry University (ZAFU), China and Nanyang Technological University, School of Biological Sciences (SBS).

  • 20th Anniversary Research Symposium

    4 August, 2022, 9 AM-5 PM
    Join us in the celebration of our achievements through this research symposium where we invite our alumni, graduate students and postdocs to share their discoveries.

  • [SBS Alumni Seminar Series] A Peek Into The Fast-Moving Consumer Goods Industry by Dr Kelvin Lee, Group Head of Procter & Gamble

    25 May, 2022, 4 PM-5 PM
    Join us at SBS Classroom 1 on 25 May (Wed), 4PM!

    9 February, 2022, 4 PM-5 PM
    Join us at SBS Classroom 1 on 9 Feb (Wed), 4PM!
